jokes.text: Johnny excitedly answers the phone 'Hello, Johnny Rose. Hello? Hello?' followed by confusion when no one responds
jokes.setup: Important business call from winery coming through
jokes.punchline: Phone issues immediately sabotage the crucial call
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff with phone issues undermining an important moment. Economical but predictable.

jokes.text: Johnny frantically yelling 'There's something wrong with the phones!' while everyone scrambles to patch the call around
jokes.setup: Phone call needs to be transferred
jokes.punchline: Complete chaos and panic over basic phone operations
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the phone chaos with character-appropriate panic from Johnny.

jokes.text: Alexis casually tells the important business caller 'No, sorry, you have the wrong room. He's actually in room 6' and hangs up
jokes.setup: Alexis is supposed to transfer the crucial call to her father
jokes.punchline: She dismisses the caller entirely, treating it like a wrong number
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Alexis's oblivious self-involvement destroying her father's business opportunity. Strong craft and character integration.

jokes.text: David dramatically asking 'Can you get a heart murmur from a lack of sleep? Because I have not slept since we've got here and I think my body is shutting down.'
jokes.setup: David appears to be having health concerns
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals it's hypochondriacal overreaction to minor discomfort
jokes.explanation: Strong character establishment - David's tendency toward hypochondria and dramatics fits perfectly.

jokes.text: Alexis casually responding 'Well, I hate to admit it, but I actually know very little about heart murmurs.'
jokes.setup: David asks medical question
jokes.punchline: Alexis's matter-of-fact admission of medical ignorance as if it's a personal failing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis line - treating medical knowledge like social trivia she's embarrassed not to know.

jokes.text: Alexis saying 'Honestly, David, I have tried to find my pulse like a thousand times and nothing. So don't worry about it.'
jokes.setup: David worrying about his pulse
jokes.punchline: Alexis casually admitting she can't find her own pulse but treating it as reassurance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - Alexis using her own inability to find vital signs as comfort for David. High craft across all metrics.

jokes.text: David's escalating health panic: 'I have not been able to find kale anywhere here- how long are we going to be talking about you for?'
jokes.setup: David listing his health concerns
jokes.punchline: Alexis interrupting his health crisis to make it about her
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy with Alexis's self-centeredness interrupting David's narcissistic health spiral.

jokes.text: David: 'I think I'm having a heart attack, is what's happening!' Alexis: 'David, you are like 34.' David: 'I'm basically 29.'
jokes.setup: Alexis dismisses David's health fears due to his age
jokes.punchline: David's immediate vanity correction even while claiming heart attack
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - David's vanity overriding his hypochondria mid-crisis. Perfect economy and character integration.

jokes.text: David's dramatic projection: 'I think you're gonna feel very guilty when I slip into a coma and you have to come visit me in the hospital at hospice.'
jokes.setup: David trying to guilt Alexis about his health fears
jokes.punchline: Combining 'hospital' and 'hospice' in dramatic but confused medical terminology
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of David's dramatics with characteristic medical confusion.

jokes.text: The ladybug in the wine: 'There's something in mine.' 'Oh, that's a ladybug. That's one of the good ones.'
jokes.setup: Moira finds something in her wine
jokes.punchline: Herb's casual dismissal of the bug as 'one of the good ones'
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag with perfect deadpan delivery that establishes the winery's questionable quality standards.

jokes.text: Moira's disgust: 'No, this tastes like something one should not put in their mouth.'
jokes.setup: Tasting the fruit wine
jokes.punchline: Moira's eloquent yet devastating review in characteristic verbose style
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific reaction with Moira's verbose way of saying the wine is terrible.

jokes.text: Moira's sudden enthusiasm: 'Like it? It's fruit wine. What's not to like?' after calling it disgusting moments earlier
jokes.setup: Moira just said the wine tastes awful
jokes.punchline: Complete 180 when offered a paying gig
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Moira's instant pivot when career opportunity arises. Strong irony and economy.

jokes.text: David's WebMD escalation: 'According to Web MD, I'm having a pulmonary embolism, which is much, much worse.'
jokes.setup: David thought he was having a heart attack
jokes.punchline: Self-diagnosis has escalated to something worse via internet research
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of David's hypochondria with relatable WebMD anxiety spiral.

jokes.text: David's existential question: 'People just drop dead in the streets out of sheer neglect?' about the town's medical care
jokes.setup: Learning there's limited medical care in town
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic assumption about rural healthcare
jokes.explanation: Good fish-out-of-water comedy with David's urban assumptions about rural life.

jokes.text: Ted's dark humor: 'Well, if it is a heart attack we're way too far away from the nearest hospital for you to make it through the night.' followed immediately by 'Kidding.'
jokes.setup: David worried about heart attack in rural location
jokes.punchline: Doctor's morbid joke followed by immediate reveal it's a joke
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with dark humor that perfectly captures small-town doctor bedside manner.

jokes.text: Dr.: 'if it is a heart attack we're way too far away from the nearest hospital for you to make it through the night. Kidding.'
jokes.setup: Doctor delivering serious medical assessment
jokes.punchline: Jokes about David dying then immediately reveals he's kidding
jokes.explanation: Well-structured misdirection joke that works with the character dynamic

jokes.text: Ted's medical question: 'Now, have you been around any feces in the past two days?' David's horrified 'I don't n...'
jokes.setup: Doctor taking medical history
jokes.punchline: David's privileged horror at the very suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing David's sheltered background clashing with basic medical questions.

jokes.text: David's dismissal of panic attacks: 'Oh no, those... Those aren't real. Those are a PR spin for celebrity publicists.'
jokes.setup: Ted diagnosing David with panic attack
jokes.punchline: David's cynical celebrity-world perspective on mental health
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific joke that reveals David's insider knowledge of celebrity PR while being ironically wrong about his own condition.

jokes.text: David's lifestyle contrast: 'I went from living in a 25 hundred square foot Soho live/workspace to a motel room with my sister.'
jokes.setup: Ted asking about recent stressors
jokes.punchline: The stark contrast between luxury and current living situation
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the show's premise with specific details that highlight the fall from grace.

jokes.text: Ted's understated response: 'Wow. Eek. That'll do it.'
jokes.setup: David's dramatic life change explanation
jokes.punchline: Ted's casual, almost childlike response to major trauma
jokes.explanation: Great understated reaction that deflates David's drama with Ted's matter-of-fact bedside manner.

jokes.text: Moira: 'You called it liquid stink this morning.' Johnny: 'The whites seem less stinky.'
jokes.setup: Johnny defending the wine he previously trashed
jokes.punchline: Forced to admit he called it 'liquid stink' but still trying to find positives
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Johnny's business-first mindset

jokes.text: Johnny critiquing wine terminology: 'Peachafication!' Who uses a word like 'peachafication'? A moron... would use that.'
jokes.setup: Reading Herb's wine literature
jokes.punchline: Johnny's indignant reaction to made-up wine terminology
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's business frustration, though 'peachafication' as a made-up word is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Moira's defensive response: 'That moron is the first moron to offer me an acting role in a very long time.'
jokes.setup: Johnny calling Herb a moron
jokes.punchline: Moira accepting the insult but defending the opportunity
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment showing Moira's desperation masked with dignity, good repetition of 'moron.'

jokes.text: Johnny complaining about the tote bag: 'But seriously, how cheap ass is that bag?' followed by Moira's disgusted reaction to handling it
jokes.setup: Examining the promotional materials
jokes.punchline: The bag is so cheap it's genuinely repulsive to touch
jokes.explanation: Decent visual gag about cheap promotional materials, though fairly predictable.

jokes.text: You have to open your eyes to get it in the bag! Ugh! Good. Ugh!
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to put away terrible wine promotional bag
jokes.punchline: Physical struggle with cheap promotional material
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy bit showing the cheap quality of promotional materials

jokes.text: Twyla asks why Alexis gets dressed up for community service, Naomi Campbell reference follows
jokes.setup: Practical question about overdressing for manual labor
jokes.punchline: Alexis's completely out-of-touch celebrity reference as justification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - absurd celebrity reference as life philosophy

jokes.text: Alexis's fashion philosophy: 'Naomi Campbell wore Dolce & Gabbana couture to her community service. And just because she was picking up roadkill did not mean that she needed to look like it.'
jokes.setup: Twyla asking why Alexis dresses up for manual labor
jokes.punchline: Bizarre celebrity fashion inspiration for inappropriate situations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment that perfectly captures Alexis's warped celebrity-influenced worldview. High craft across all metrics.

jokes.text: Twyla's confused response: 'Um... I didn't understand a word you just said.'
jokes.setup: Alexis's elaborate fashion philosophy
jokes.punchline: Twyla's honest confusion at Alexis's reference points
jokes.explanation: Good straight-person reaction that grounds Alexis's absurdity in relatable confusion.

jokes.text: Patrick's self-deprecating humor about his wardrobe: 'When a guy can pack his whole wardrobe into an overnight bag, you just don't really think that he'd be much of an interior designer.'
jokes.setup: Commenting on his minimalist style
jokes.punchline: Connecting wardrobe size to design expectations
jokes.explanation: Decent self-aware humor that establishes Patrick's practical nature versus David's aesthetic focus.

jokes.text: Patrick's literal laundry method: 'It's just I do beat my laundry against rocks.' David: 'You're a freak.'
jokes.setup: Patrick talking about 'rock-beaten freshness'
jokes.punchline: Reveals he actually beats laundry against rocks
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment that reveals Patrick's primitive living conditions while maintaining his positive attitude.

jokes.text: Johnny's film knowledge pretension on set: 'What uh... what kind of lens are we using here?' 'Any primes? Using any primes?'
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to be helpful on commercial set
jokes.punchline: Using film jargon to seem knowledgeable when he's out of his depth
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Johnny's need to seem expert in areas where he's clearly not.

jokes.text: Alexis dismissing panic attacks: 'Babe, you know that panic attacks aren't real, right? They're just things that celebrities make up to...'
jokes.setup: David explaining his panic attack diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Alexis using David's exact same dismissive logic against him
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows the siblings' shared cynical worldview while creating perfect irony.

jokes.text: David's selfish system explanation: 'I don't wanna have to bring this up... But it's my turn to take a selfish.' Reference to Dubai 2010 disaster
jokes.setup: David wanting Alexis to skip yoga for him
jokes.punchline: Revealing they have a formal system for selfishness with precedent
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character worldbuilding that reveals how the siblings have systematized their selfishness. High craft and character integration.

jokes.text: Johnny's onset interference causing him to be sent home: 'You're making me nervous. And apparently you're making everyone nervous.'
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to help with Moira's commercial
jokes.punchline: His 'help' is so disruptive he gets banned from set
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's inability to step back even when trying to help.

jokes.text: David and Alexis at yoga: 'Okay, well, I'm not touching you.' 'Well, I'm not touching you.'
jokes.setup: Partner yoga requires them to work together
jokes.punchline: Immediate mutual revulsion at the idea of touching
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic humor, though the joke structure is fairly basic.

jokes.text: Jocelyn's oversharing: 'Normally I do this with Roland, but he had a bad burrito so he's M.I.A.' 'Trust me, that's good news for everybody.'
jokes.setup: Explaining Roland's absence from yoga
jokes.punchline: TMI about Roland's digestive issues and casual dismissal of husband
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Jocelyn's oversharing tendency and marriage dynamics.

jokes.text: Jocelyn: 'Roland had a bad burrito so he's M.I.A. Trust me, that's good news for everybody.'
jokes.setup: Explaining partner yoga arrangements
jokes.punchline: Everyone's relief that Roland's digestive issues keep him away
jokes.explanation: Standard but well-executed joke about an unpleasant character

jokes.text: Mutt revealing David's 'nervous breakdown' diagnosis: 'Ted told me all about your... Nervous breakdown.' 'When he said it was someone "precious," I knew.'
jokes.setup: David's medical visit was supposed to be private
jokes.punchline: Small town gossip and Ted's coded description identifying David
jokes.explanation: Good small-town comedy with the 'precious' descriptor perfectly identifying David's personality.

jokes.text: Moira's wine commercial takes: 'glace' instead of 'glass,' 'flute line' instead of 'fruit wine,' 'moina rose' instead of 'Moira Rose'
jokes.setup: Simple wine commercial filming
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly ridiculous verbal flubs
jokes.explanation: Good escalating comedy sequence, though verbal flubs are somewhat predictable for nervous actor storyline.

jokes.text: Producer's harsh ultimatum: 'that Bitch gets her Ass out of the trailer' reported by crew member
jokes.setup: Moira has locked herself in bathroom after breakdown
jokes.punchline: Blunt, crude assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation but relies somewhat on crude language for impact rather than cleverness.

jokes.text: Jocelyn's sex advice during yoga: 'Um, have you tried sex?' 'Sex puts me right to sleep. You should have lots and lots of sex.'
jokes.setup: David discussing his sleep/anxiety issues
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's overly direct sexual advice in yoga setting
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jocelyn's lack of boundaries, though the humor is fairly straightforward.

jokes.text: Alexis accidentally touching Mutt inappropriately during yoga: 'Omigod! Omigod! I'm so sorry. I'm sssso sorry.' with exaggerated panic
jokes.setup: Partner yoga poses creating awkward physical contact
jokes.punchline: Alexis's over-the-top reaction to accidental inappropriate contact
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup with predictable outcome, though Alexis's reaction is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Moira's drunken door opening method: 'How did you open the door?' 'I threw a shoe.'
jokes.setup: Johnny asking how she opened the door
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous solution revealing her drunken state
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment with perfect economy - reveals character state and problem-solving in three words.

jokes.text: Moira: 'I can't feel my tongue. But I know it's there because I'm talking.'
jokes.setup: Moira drinking away her commercial disaster
jokes.punchline: Drunk logic about tongue sensation
jokes.explanation: Excellent drunk logic delivered with Moira's characteristic precision

jokes.text: Johnny's confession about being drunk on Sunrise Bay: 'You were drunk most of season three... And half of season four... End of season five.'
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to encourage Moira by referencing past success
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing extensive drinking problem during her show
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with good escalating structure that recontextualizes Moira's past 'success.'

jokes.text: Johnny's motivational speech: 'You're radiant. You're beguiling. You're daytime television's brightest star!'
jokes.setup: Trying to build Moira's confidence
jokes.punchline: Overwrought praise for daytime TV work as if it's high art
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's supportive but somewhat delusional perspective on Moira's career.

jokes.text: Yoga confessions: 'I burnt my sausage casserole.' 'I'm pretty sure I'm really lonely here.'
jokes.setup: Yoga instructor asking to identify tensions
jokes.punchline: Mundane and profound confessions delivered equally
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between trivial and meaningful problems, with good character-appropriate responses.

jokes.text: Moira's final wine commercial with completely mangled winery names: 'Herb Erfling... ger. Burt Herngeif. Irv Herb-blinger. Bing Livehaanger. Liveling. Burt Herkurn. Ban-Bingo ling-!'
jokes.setup: Moira finally attempting to nail the commercial
jokes.punchline: Complete breakdown into nonsensical name variations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating wordplay sequence that perfectly caps Moira's commercial disaster. Highly quotable and rewardingly absurd.
